description:
Advances: mutagen.coverage, mutagen.mutation_pipeline

Deliverable:
- `lib/mutagen_ex/coverage_runner.ex` (module `MutagenEx.CoverageRunner`)
  implementing [mutagen.coverage](.spec/specs/coverage.spec.md) r1-r5, r7.
  Aborts cleanly if cover already running. Forces `max_cases: 1` and
  configured `seed`. Stops cover idempotently in `try/rescue`.
- `lib/mutagen_ex/baseline.ex` (module `MutagenEx.Baseline`) implementing
  [mutagen.mutation_pipeline](.spec/specs/mutation_pipeline.spec.md) r1, r2.
  Aborts pipeline on red baseline; emits the failures list.
- `lib/mutagen_ex/ast_cache.ex` (module `MutagenEx.AstCache`) implementing
  [mutagen.coverage](.spec/specs/coverage.spec.md) r6. Single-load,
  immutable, keeps verbatim source text alongside the AST.
- `lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner.ex` (module `MutagenEx.MutationRunner`)
  implementing [mutagen.mutation_pipeline](.spec/specs/mutation_pipeline.spec.md) r3-r11.
  Refuses self-mutation; classifies into 5 outcomes; restores via
  `Code.compile_quoted/1` on cached AST; emits state-drift warning for
  `use SomeModule` modules.
- `lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner/mutation_loop.ex` (private module
  `MutagenEx.MutationRunner.MutationLoop`) per [mutagen.decision.mutation_loop_private](.spec/decisions/mutation_loop_private.md).
  Owns per-mutation timeout via `Task.async + Task.yield + Task.shutdown(:brutal_kill)`,
  stdout/stderr suppression, and state snapshotting.
- Integration tests at `test/mutagen_ex/coverage_runner_test.exs`,
  `.../baseline_test.exs`, `.../ast_cache_test.exs`, `.../mutation_runner_test.exs`.
  All use `on_exit/1` for `:cover.stop` defensive cleanup (per H-Tt3).

Verification:
- Tests to pass: `mix test --include integration test/mutagen_ex/`.
  Covers spec-verification stubs `mutagen.coverage.v1`-`.v3`,
  `mutagen.mutation_pipeline.v1`, `.v2`.
- Behaviors to demonstrate: scenarios `mutagen.coverage.s1` through `s7`,
  scenarios `mutagen.mutation_pipeline.s1` through `s8`.
- The C1 and C2 spike tests from S2 must still pass; they are regression
  for the primary state-hygiene invariants this stage implements.
- The implementer must run S5's test suite at least 3 consecutive times
  locally before declaring merge-gate-green; any flake = state-hygiene bug.

Out of Scope (files):
- `lib/mutagen_ex/json_reporter.ex` (S6)
- `lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex` orchestration body (S6 wires it; this stage
  only fills out the dispatch-table callees)
- `test/fixtures/lane_project/**` (S7)
- Anything under `test/mutagen_ex/golden/`

Allowed touches (this stage may modify):
- `lib/mutagen_ex/coverage_runner.ex`
- `lib/mutagen_ex/baseline.ex`
- `lib/mutagen_ex/ast_cache.ex`
- `lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner.ex`
- `lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner/mutation_loop.ex`
- `test/mutagen_ex/coverage_runner_test.exs`
- `test/mutagen_ex/baseline_test.exs`
- `test/mutagen_ex/ast_cache_test.exs`
- `test/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner_test.exs`

Out of Scope (intent):
- Do not promote `MutationLoop` to a peer module. It is private to the
  runner per [mutagen.decision.mutation_loop_private](.spec/decisions/mutation_loop_private.md).
- Do not add per-mutation linked-process tracking via `Process.monitor/1`.
  v1 accepts the coarse `Process.registered()` signal — finer tracking is
  gold-plating.
- Do not introduce a "3 consecutive timeouts → hard abort" branch.
  Per [mutagen.decision.timeout_handling](.spec/decisions/timeout_handling.md),
  each timeout is `:timeout` in `mutation.results` and the runner continues.
- Do not introduce a `--strict-restore` flag. The state-drift warning in
  the JSON is the only signal v1 ships.
- Do not retry red-baseline tests. The judge can re-invoke the tool.

Merge gate:
- [ ] Code compiles cleanly with `--warnings-as-errors`
- [ ] Integration test suite passes 3× consecutively
- [ ] S2 spike tests still pass
- [ ] Spec verification passes (`mutagen.coverage.v1`-`.v3`, `mutagen.mutation_pipeline.v1`, `.v2`)
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs (mechanical)
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket
title: S5: Coverage + Baseline + AstCache + MutationRunner + MutationLoop
